prep.summary: You and Jonathan are deep into two parallel tracks — Project Genie (AI-driven Jira/acceptance criteria automation) and Architecture as Code (three-tier data model with Business, Architecture, and Specification layers). The 5/14 meeting landed on YAML-based data management, GenAI Council governance entry, and a push toward a full 'Business Analyst Coworker' AI; the 5/11 meeting flagged the TDSR lightweight app pilot with Cody, alignment needed with Osterkamp's OurDoc roadmap, and a desire to resurrect Architecture Recommendations as a formal capability. Outstanding threads include handing Genie's governance into the GenAI Council queue, engineering hand-off of IPD questionnaire onboarding, and the stakeholder meeting with Osterkamp and McCarty to move Architecture as Code from vacuum to MVP.
tasks: Confirm whether the GenAI Council submission for Project Genie has been queued — and if not, identify who owns that action and by when.
Review the status of the IPD engineering hand-off — determine which engineers are assigned and whether questionnaire onboarding has begun.
Get a status update on the Osterkamp/McCarty alignment meeting — establish whether it has been scheduled and what the MVP scope looks like.
Clarify where the TDSR lightweight app pilot stands — check if Jonathan Stewart's business and architecture specs are finalized and whether Osterkamp's OurDoc roadmap conflict has been resolved.
Verify that the YAML/taxonomy pipeline work from 5/14 is on track — confirm whether HTML generation is now being built directly from YAML or still running off the temporary workaround.
prep.title: Meeting with Jonathan

schedule_html:

openwork_html: Mark's list Reach out to the first Paver guy for more information Due today
